notes: Presentation, search, and public-export surfaces are downstream projections rather than canonical storage.
Append-only sidecars preserve correction, evidence, and review lineage around canonical graph records.

canonical_record_families.description: Canonical people, groups, places, works, and detected local entities after reconciliation.
canonical_record_families.provenance_strategy: Link each entity to one or more provenance_event rows and preserve source-local identifiers separately.
canonical_record_families.review_strategy: Record-level review_state plus field-review-state sidecars for durable labels, identifiers, and scoped facts.

canonical_record_families.description: Directed, source-backed links between canonical entities and scoped subject assignments.
canonical_record_families.provenance_strategy: Preserve predicate registry semantics and evidence references for each asserted edge.
canonical_record_families.review_strategy: Review queue refs and correction-ledger supersession preserve edge lineage without destructive overwrite.

canonical_record_families.description: Source-backed claims and extracted statements that may later support entity or relationship promotion.
canonical_record_families.provenance_strategy: Retain source-backed claim provenance and link field-level evidence snapshots through evidence locators.
canonical_record_families.review_strategy: Field-review-state determines whether assertion content can contribute to authoritative downstream surfaces.

canonical_record_families.description: Append-only operational and object-level events that explain discovery, import, review, merge, and export actions.
canonical_record_families.provenance_strategy: This family is the provenance substrate for the rest of the canonical graph.
canonical_record_families.review_strategy: Events remain append-only; review applies to the canonical records they describe rather than mutating historical events.

canonical_record_families.description: Normalized confidence posture attached to entities, relationships, assertions, and topic extensions without erasing original score context.
canonical_record_families.provenance_strategy: Confidence values keep source and tool context and may point back to the provenance events that produced them.
canonical_record_families.review_strategy: Confidence alone never promotes material; reviewed canonical records may still retain lower-confidence history.

canonical_record_families.description: Operator review, demotion, ambiguity, and acceptance posture for canonical graph records and record fields.
canonical_record_families.provenance_strategy: Review annotations link back to reviewed object refs and related provenance events when available.
canonical_record_families.review_strategy: Record review history and field-review-state sidecars are append-only and remain distinct from canonical entity content.

append_only_sidecars.purpose: Preserve dedupe, merge, split, and supersession lineage over canonical graph object refs.
Preserve append-only review posture for durable fields and authoritative downstream decisions.
Preserve stable evidence spans and redaction posture without turning presentation excerpts into canonical storage.

migration_stages.title: Map current durable SQLite families into canonical record families
migration_stages.notes: This stage is non-destructive and documents canonical ownership without changing current runtime tables.

migration_stages.title: Treat correction, evidence, and field review as first-class sidecars to canonical graph records
migration_stages.notes: Downstream search and publication surfaces should read these sidecars instead of embedding ad hoc review and evidence state.

migration_stages.title: Use the canonical graph layer as the substrate for feedback loops and future read models
migration_stages.notes: Later runtime work such as candidate feedback and public-safe projections should consume canonical graph records rather than importer-specific row shapes.
